author: catwith1hat
content: It's a bit more structured, but there is a METHODOLOGY.md in there, which is outdated but kinda still gives the picture. Once the agent has enough context, it can generate investigation items reasonably well. Then I had subagents move the investigation items into a structured form and write test code around it to confirm testing hypothesis. At the end, I had git presubmit hammer the individual investigation items into a rigid form, so that I could generate the main README.md tables and ITEM_TOC.md from the forced included metadata

author: barnabasbusa
content:
```yaml
participants_matrix:
  el:
    - el_type: geth
      el_image: ethpandaops/geth:bal-devnet-6

  cl:
    - cl_type: lighthouse
      cl_image: ethpandaops/lighthouse:glamsterdam-devnet-3
    - cl_type: prysm
      cl_image: ethpandaops/prysm-beacon-chain:glamsterdam-devnet-3
    - cl_type: lodestar
      cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:glamsterdam-devnet-3
    - cl_type: grandine
      cl_image: ethpandaops/grandine:glamsterdam-devnet-2

  count: 1

network_params:
  preset: mainnet
  gloas_fork_epoch: 0
```

author: terencechain
content:
i think we have a bug, ill leave it here <@755590043632140352> <@360491619402776577> if you want to review it
we are stuck / cant propose a block because of this
we first have a long reorg:
```
  Chain reorg occurred commonAncestorRoot=0xb2d5ea02 depth=12 distance=21
    newRoot=0xb15b2422 newSlot=65476 oldRoot=0xe7f49c17 oldSlot=65479
```
then geth vs prysm head are:
```
  - Prysm CL: head = #48688 (0xbcfc5a)
  - Geth EL: head = #48691 (0x189d60)
```
for some reason we are 3 behind.. then every FCU+ attribute call we got this from geth:
```
Ignoring beacon update to old head
```
then from prysm we got:
```
Engine did not return a payload ID for the fork choice update with attributes
```
The fact that we are 3 behind for head execution payload is odd, i dont know how we ended up in such situation
